question: Based on Table 5.2-2 RSV specifications and Figure 5.2-1, is the RSV installation adequate for reliable overpressure protection?

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale’s compliance with NRC regulations and ASME codes for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary. It details the use of specific ASME BPVC and OM code cases for design, fabrication, inspection, and testing, ensuring all selected code cases meet regulatory requirements and conditions as specified in relevant NRC regulatory guides. Table 5.2-1 consolidates the specific code cases applied.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life. It details the components included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, all designed in accordance with regulatory definitions and requirements. The section also references related systems and components that contribute to maintaining RCPB integrity.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It confirms that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III, 2013 Edition standards, with appropriate quality classifications and adherence to NRC regulatory guides.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards relevant to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases related to materials, quality assurance, and inspection procedures for nuclear facility applications.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the document for regulatory purposes, and any copies must retain the copyright notice. The section also references the table of contents for Chapter Five, indicating the structure and topics covered.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components in the NuScale reactor, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) and lists the applicable material specifications for components such as the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, and bolting.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing continuous monitoring and control.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes key components of the NuScale reactor system. It details the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core and supports major systems, and outlines the external Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping and its connections. The section also introduces the pressurizer (PZR), which is integral to the RPV, and references further detailed descriptions in subsequent sections.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design emphasizes maintaining coolant quality, uniform boron concentration, and preventing fuel design limit violations during all operating conditions. Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) contains its own RCS, and the design bases focus on safety, performance, and minimizing solid deposits in the system.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across various power levels by establishing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures, using both software and manual calculations. It details how uncertainties and design considerations are addressed to ensure efficient operation, maximize electrical generation, and maintain safety margins, particularly emphasizing the low pressure losses that support the plant’s natural circulation design.

edges.description: Section A describes compliance with ASME and NRC regulatory requirements for overpressure protection, which are necessary to ensure the design features in Section B maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

Section A describes the overpressure protection provisions and references compliance with ASME and NRC requirements, while Section B specifies that the design and construction meet those same regulatory codes, thus Section B provides the regulatory basis that Section A claims to satisfy.

Section A discusses compliance with ASME and NRC regulatory requirements for overpressure protection, while Section B lists the specific industry codes and standards referenced to support those compliance claims.

Section A describes the design features intended to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B states that these features comply with specific regulatory codes and standards, connecting the design implementation (A) with regulatory compliance (B).

Section A describes the design features and integrity requirements for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B lists references that provide supporting standards and guidelines for those requirements, connecting them through the use of referenced technical criteria.

Section A asserts compliance with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements for reactor coolant boundary components, while Section B lists references including the ASME NQA-1 quality assurance standard that supports the compliance claims in Section A.

Section A provides the context and scope of the document, while Section B details the regulatory codes and standards (such as ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a) that the RSV installation must comply with for overpressure protection, thus Section A is a prerequisite for understanding the regulatory framework discussed in Section B.

Section A provides introductory and copyright information for the NuScale Standard Plant application, while Section B specifies that the reactor coolant pressure boundary (including RSV installation) complies with regulatory requirements (10 CFR 50.55a) and ASME BPVC Section III, thus connecting the design documentation to its regulatory compliance.

Section A provides detailed descriptions of pressurizer components and their materials, which are part of the reactor coolant system, while Section B outlines the regulatory requirements and code compliance for overpressure protection, connecting them through the need for the pressurizer's design and materials to meet the overpressure protection codes.
Section A specifies the materials and components of the pressurizer, which are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary described in Section B as essential for maintaining system integrity.

Section A describes the materials and components of the pressurizer, which are part of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B states that these components comply with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC Class 1 requirements, thus connecting the design details in A to the regulatory compliance in B.

Section B references Section 5.2, which is the subject of the document introduced in Section A, establishing a structural connection between the referenced material specifications and the broader document context.

Section A describes the design and function of the pressurizer, which is directly relevant to Section B's discussion of overpressure protection requirements, as the pressurizer's configuration and operation must satisfy the regulatory codes and standards referenced in Section B for adequate overpressure protection.

Section A describes the pressurizer's design and function as part of the reactor vessel, which is a pressure-retaining component directly referenced in Section B's definition of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) whose integrity must be maintained.

Section A describes the design and function of the pressurizer, a pressure-retaining component, while Section B states that such components comply with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, connecting the pressurizer's design to its regulatory compliance.

Section A describes operational steps and system preparations involving the RSV and related components, while Section B outlines the regulatory and code compliance requirements (ASME and 10 CFR) that the RSV installation must meet for overpressure protection, connecting operational adequacy to regulatory standards.

Section A describes operational steps and system manipulations (such as venting and isolation) that directly affect the pressure and integrity of the reactor coolant boundary discussed in Section B, thereby providing operational context for maintaining the RCPB as required by regulatory definitions.

Section A describes operational steps and system component functions relevant to pressure management, while Section B states that these components, including the RSV, are designed and constructed to meet regulatory codes for pressure boundary integrity, thus connecting the operational adequacy to regulatory compliance.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system design, which includes components relevant to overpressure protection, while Section B details the regulatory codes and standards (such as ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a) that the system, including the RSV installation, must comply with for overpressure protection.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system (RCS), which includes the components relevant to overpressure protection, while Section B specifies the requirement to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), which is directly affected by the adequacy of the RSV installation described in Table 5.2-2 and Figure 5.2-1.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor coolant system and its components, while Section B specifies that these components, including the RSV installation, comply with regulatory codes and standards for design and construction.

Section A discusses the system evaluation criteria for optimal performance, which includes establishing steady state parameters that are relevant for overpressure protection, while Section B outlines the regulatory and code compliance requirements (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that the RSV installation must meet for reactor coolant boundary integrity; thus, Section A's design results must satisfy Section B's regulatory requirements for overpressure protection adequacy.

Section A discusses the system evaluation criteria, including establishing steady state parameters for optimal performance, which directly impact the design and operation of overpressure protection systems like the RSV, while Section B outlines the requirement to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which the RSV installation must satisfy to ensure reliable overpressure protection.

Section A discusses the system evaluation and performance criteria for the NuScale Power Plant, while Section B establishes that the reactor coolant pressure boundary components, including those relevant to overpressure protection, comply with regulatory codes (10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC), thus Section A's design results are intended to satisfy Section B's regulatory requirements.

Section A describes specific operational steps and component interactions (such as PZR water level adjustment and venting) that are part of the broader RCS described in Section B, thus providing detailed implementation for the general system overview in Section B.

Section A describes the operational steps and component manipulations (such as isolating the SGs and adjusting PZR water level) that set up the system for overpressure protection, while Section B outlines the evaluation criteria and steady-state parameter establishment that assess whether these operational setups (from Section A) result in optimal and reliable system performance, thus connecting the practical setup to its adequacy evaluation.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system's design, while Section B evaluates the system's performance, including how the design described in A meets operational criteria relevant to overpressure protection.
